name: Pic Saint Barthélemy – hike starting from the Moulzoune lake car park
name_original: Le Pic Saint Barthélemy – randonnée au départ du parking du lac de Moulzoune
region_intro: Hiking around Montferrier offers access to the dramatic Pyrenean terrain of the Ariège department, characterized by mountainous landscapes and diverse trails. The region features prominent peaks like the Massif de Tabe, including Pic de Saint-Barthélemy, and picturesque lakes such as Étang de Moulzoune and Étang d'Appy. Deep gorges like the Gorges de la Frau, carved by the Èrç river, add to the varied natural environment. Trails wind through forests, across ridges, and past historical sites, providing a range of experiences. Best hiking trails around Montferrier The most popular hiking route is Pic Saint Barthélemy – hike starting from the Moulzoune lake car park, a 9.2 miles (14.9 km) trail that takes 6 hours 26 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers magnificent views of the Ariège Pyrenees, including the Montcalm and Bassiès Massifs. Another top favourite among local hikers is View of Étang d'Appy – Appy Pond loop from Les Monts d'Olmes, a difficult 6.3 miles (10.1 km) path. This trail leads to serene natural settings around the lake, serving as a scenic stop for longer excursions. Local hikers also love the Montségur – Celtic cross loop from Le Carrial, a 14.7 miles (23.6 km) trail leading through historical sites including the famous Cathar castle, often completed in about 7 hours 40 minutes. Hiking around Montferrier is defined by mountainous Pyrenean terrain, picturesque lakes, and historical landmarks. The network offers options for various ability levels, from moderate walks to strenuous long-distance treks. The routes in Montferrier are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 250 reviews. More than 2000 hikers have used komoot to explore Montferrier's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What types of hiking trails can I expect around Montferrier?
A: Montferrier offers a diverse range of hiking experiences within the dramatic Pyrenean terrain of the Ariège department. You'll find trails winding through dense forests, across open grassy ridges, and past significant historical sites. The landscape is characterized by deep, steep valleys and jagged ridges, providing varied scenery from moderate walks to strenuous long-distance treks.

Q: Are there any easy or moderate hiking options in Montferrier?
A: Yes, Montferrier caters to various fitness levels. While many trails are challenging, there are 8 easy and 14 moderate routes available. For a moderate option, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/15022844" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mount Fourcat loop from Les Monts d'Olmes&lt;/a&gt;, which offers rewarding views without the extreme difficulty of some higher peaks.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in the Montferrier area?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Montferrier are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1475960" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Étang d'Appy – Appy Pond loop from Les Monts d'Olmes&lt;/a&gt; or the moderate &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/36013515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Shepherd's Hut – Cabane du Taulat loop from SN 1678&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on hikes near Montferrier?
A: Yes, the region boasts natural wonders like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7350054" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Roquefort-les-Cascades Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt;, which are particularly impressive after rainfall. These can be a beautiful highlight to incorporate into your hiking plans.

Q: What historical sites can I explore while hiking in Montferrier?
A: Montferrier is rich in history, notably with the famous Cathar castle of Montségur. You can hike to this iconic site, for example, via the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38592967" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Montségur – Celtic cross loop from Le Carrial&lt;/a&gt;. The area is also part of longer historical routes like the 'Camí dels Bons Homes' (GR-107) and the Cathar Trail (GR®367).

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints or natural monuments to visit?
A: Absolutely. The region offers breathtaking panoramic views from peaks like Pic de Saint-Barthélemy. You can also explore natural monuments such as the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/456262" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Gorges de la Frau&lt;/a&gt;, a three-kilometer-long gorge carved by the Èrç river, or the unique &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3407492" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fontestorbes Spring&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails around Montferrier?
A: The hiking trails around Montferrier are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 250 reviews. Hikers often praise the magnificent views from peaks like Pic Saint-Barthélemy and the serene natural settings around lakes such as Étang d'Appy.

Q: Are there any lakes accessible via hiking trails in Montferrier?
A: Yes, the region features picturesque lakes that are often integrated into hiking routes. Notable examples include Étang de Moulzoune and Étang d'Appy, which offer serene natural settings and serve as scenic stops. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e956895389" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pic Saint Barthélemy hike&lt;/a&gt;, for instance, starts from the Moulzoune lake car park.

Q: Is it possible to reach hiking trailheads in Montferrier by public transport?
A: While specific public transport options to every trailhead can vary, Montferrier is located in the Ariège department, which has some regional bus services. For detailed information on bus routes and schedules to specific starting points, it's advisable to check local transport authority websites or inquire locally before your trip.

Q: Are there good parking facilities near the hiking trails in Montferrier?
A: Many popular trailheads, especially those leading to significant peaks or lakes, typically have designated parking areas. For example, the hike to Pic Saint Barthélemy often starts from the Moulzoune lake car park. It's always a good idea to check the specific route details on komoot for parking information at your chosen starting point.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Montferrier?
A: The best time to hike in Montferrier generally depends on the altitude and difficulty of the chosen trail. Spring and autumn offer pleasant temperatures and vibrant scenery, while summer is ideal for higher altitude treks. Winter hiking is possible but requires appropriate gear and experience due to snow and ice conditions in the Pyrenees.

Q: Are there any long-distance hiking routes that pass through or near Montferrier?
A: Yes, Montferrier is situated near significant long-distance hiking routes. The challenging 'Camí dels Bons Homes' (GR-107) traverses the Pyrenees, and the Cathar Trail (GR®367) connects Port la Nouvelle to Foix, offering moderate to difficult sections in the Ariège region. These trails provide opportunities for multi-day adventures.
tips: The Etang des Truites is a magnificent site which can be either a step towards the Pic Saint-Barthélemy for the more athletic, or a more accessible hiking objective.

The Devil's pond can be either a stopover towards the Pic Saint-Barthélemy for the more athletic, or an objective for those who hike to the Trout pond and want to push a little towards another magnificent lake.
Small pond at altitude towards the Pic Saint Barthélemy.
The peak of Saint-Barthélemy in the Tabe massif culminates at 2,348 m and offers a magnificent view of the massif.
